entry.title: MK Studios Announces Key West International Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. MK Studios has recently announced the development and imminent release of Key West International Airport (KEYW) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, serving Key West, Florida, with an average of 1.5 million passengers per year. The airport’s history goes back over a century, when Augustin Parla flew to Cuba in 1913. Fifteen years later, Pan American Airways began flying scheduled routes from Key West. It was briefly taken over by the US Army during World War 2 after the Pearl Harbor attack, as an alternative to the Trumbo Point seaplane base and the main Naval Air Station for fixed-wing aircraft. ​ It was returned to the city after the war, leading to its application for FAA grants in 1953 and its designation as Key West International Airport. Allegiant Air, American Airlines, American Eagle, Avelo Airlines, Breeze Airways, Delta Air Lines, Delta Connection, JetBlue, Spirit Airlines, United Airlines, and United Express serve it. The scenery will feature custom ground textures, an up-to-date ground layout, custom ground service equipment, custom terminal interiors, custom jetways, and more. ​ There’s no information on the release date or pricing yet, but Threshold will keep you informed. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: A2A Simulations Announces Accu-Sim Legacy RG550 for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. A2A Simulations has recently announced the development of their third airplane for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024: the Legacy RG550, a high-performance experimental kit-built aircraft! It’s the first time A2A Simulations has developed a kit-built airplane, which poses a significant challenge because no two kit-built aircraft are identical. But they claim it’s “perfect” for their Accu-Sim lineup because that individuality is the essence of their digital ownership philosophy. ​ The reference unit at hand is based on a real airplane owned and flown by Scott Gentile (A2A’s founder) for a number of years, allowing them to intricately replicate its flight dynamics, behavior, and quirks, just like the Comanche 250 and the Aerostar 600 (which they also happen to own, although the Aerostar 600 is no longer under their ownership). ​ The add-on will feature fully custom flight physics, a custom sound design, persistent wear and tear, multiple avionics configurations (including a full custom Garmin G5 simulation that will be retrofitted into the Comanche and Aerostar), laser-scanned and photogrammetry assisted 3D modeling, in-depth electrical simulation, fully simulated systems, and a highly detailed engine simulation model, down to individual sparks during combustion. ​ It’s scheduled for release in Q2 2026, and pricing is yet to be announced (though it will likely fall in the usual $49.99 ballpark). If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: FSLabs Updates A321ceo/neo for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FSLabs has recently issued an update to their A321ceo and neo for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, focusing on performance, stability, and overall refinement. Update v11.0.1.326 is the first to be released since the Pratt & Whitney update rolled out a few weeks ago, addressing the high VRAM usage issues reported by many users through a multi-phase optimization process, which started with an option to entirely disable the cabin seats, and now features a multi-stage cabin and model optimization which substantially reduces the memory and load footprint, delivering memory reductions without any compromises to the overall visual quality. Furthermore, they also worked on handling, refining the braking behavior with updated pedal curves, with a more accurate and progressive deceleration feel. Plans are in place to address slow-speed stopping behavior in future updates. They also resolved SRS and LOC capture issues that were plaguing the neos. There were multiple system-level improvements across engines, FADEC, EIU, DMC, and supporting systems, based on customer feedback and internal validation efforts. The FMGC stability has also been improved, alongside a more robust logging system to help detect and diagnose edge-case issues. ​ The FSLabs A321ceo and neo are available on their website for $69.95 + VAT each. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Simulación Extrema Releases El Chaltén Aeroclub for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Simulación Extrema has recently released El Chaltén Aeroclub (LAD 5138) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing a small but vital Patagonian airfield to the sim. Set beneath the towering Andes and the iconic Mount Fitz Roy, the gravel-and-dirt strip serves as a key link for tourism, medical flights, training, and firefighting operations in Argentina’s trekking capital. The scenery features hand-crafted 3D models of the aeroclub facilities, custom runway and apron textures, a detailed Mount Fitz Roy model, and static local wildlife, such as guanacos and rheas. Four parking positions are included, with one near runway 27 set up to simulate firefighting water-loading operations, reflecting the field’s real-world role during forest fire season. Read more here. El Chaltén is available now on the MSFS Marketplace for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Guilb’Air Teases Cannes Quai du Large Helistation for XP12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. A new X-Plane 12 scenery has been teased, with developer Guilb’Air revealing an upcoming rendition of Cannes Quai du Large Helistation (FR-0200). Located along the waterfront in Cannes, in the Alpes-Maritimes region of southern France, Quai du Large is a compact but demanding heliport, positioned right beside the marina, and just minutes from the city centre. You can watch a preview stream here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Laminar Research Releases X-Plane 12.4.0
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Laminar Research has officially pushed X-Plane 12.4.0 out of beta, with the update now available to both LR Direct and Steam users. Following a lengthy public testing phase, 12.4.0 arrives as a substantial platform update, focusing on performance refinements, aircraft system improvements, and expanded hardware support. One of the headline additions is the introduction of multi-threading for scenery loading. X-Plane can now distribute scenery preparation tasks across multiple CPU cores, reducing bottlenecks when operating in dense or complex environments. For those flying into detailed airports, large cities, or custom scenery-heavy regions, this change should help improve overall smoothness, particularly during approach, taxi, and initial loading phases. Modern multi-core systems, of course, stand to benefit the most from this update. The default Airbus A330-300 has received a significant round of updates. Electrical systems have been refined, avionics logic improved, and cockpit display behaviour adjusted for greater accuracy. ACARS functionality is now also accessible via the third MCDU. The Cirrus SR22, among others, sees avionics tweaks, improved autopilot logic, and clearer navigation display behaviour. X-Plane 12.4.0 also introduces native Windows support for the Tobii Eye Tracker 5. You can review the complete 12.4.0 release notes here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Verticalsim Releases Palm Beach International Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Verticalsim has recently released their rendition of Palm Beach International Airport (KPBI) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, serving the Miami and Port St. Lucie metropolitan areas, with an average of 6.6 million passengers per year. It was opened in 1936 as Morrison Field, eventually becoming an important training airfield during World War 2 and operating as a staging base for the Allied invasion of France in 1944, with aircraft departing Palm Beach towards England in preparation for D-Day. ‍After a brief return to civilian use, the airport was once again used for military purposes in the early 1950s as Palm Beach Air Force Base, training nearly 23,000 airmen during the Korean War. The Air Force Base was closed in 1962, and Palm Beach was back to being just a civilian airport. Currently, the airport has 32 gates and three concourses. Concourse A houses Bahamasair and Silver Airways; B houses Air Canada, American Airlines, Southwest Airlines, Sun Country Airlines, and United Airlines; C, on the other hand, holds Delta, Frontier, JetBlue, and Spirit. The scenery features an accurate rendition of the airport, with an up-to-date ground layout, a highly detailed terminal with a fully modeled interior, performance-friendly optimization, custom taxiway signage, true-to-life landside, and much more. It’s available on their website for roughly $21.99, requiring at least 3.67 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍ ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Announces Hong Kong International Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently announced the development of Hong Kong International Airport (VHHH) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, the 8th busiest airport in the World, with an average of 71 million passengers per year. Hong Kong International was built to replace the old Kai Tak Airport, deemed too small to handle the traffic it was handling, and to address the insane number of complaints from airlines due to the delays, a direct consequence of the fact that it could not scale up to the demand. Planning studies started in 1974, with construction work only kicking in almost 20 years later, in 1991. Completion was achieved in early 1998. Built on a large artificial island, the total area of the airport (12.48 square kilometers) corresponds to nearly 1% of Hong Kong’s total area. The airport is a hub for Cathay Pacific, Greater Bay Express, HK Express, and Hong Kong Airlines, and is visited by over 100 other airlines with more than 180 destinations. It’s also the World’s busiest cargo airport, hosting Air Hong Kong, Cathay Cargo, DHL Aviation, Hong Kong Airlines Cargo, Kalitta Air, Polar Air Cargo, and UPS Airlines. The scenery will feature an up-to-date ground layout, true-to-life landside, handcrafted ground textures, custom jetways, highly detailed terminals, performance-optimized performance, custom night lighting, and a Point of Interest pack for Hong Kong landmarks. There’s no pricing or release date information just yet, but Threshold will keep you informed! If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Public Voting Now Open for the Flight Sim Awards 2026
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. Public voting for the Flight Sim Awards 2026 is now open! This is your chance to vote for your favourite products, developers, publishers and creators that had an impact on your flight simulation experience in 2025. We invite you to spend 5-10 minutes casting your vote, because every vote matters. The nomination process combined input from the community survey, content creators, media outlets, and the Flight Sim Awards organising board and jury. This approach was designed to ensure that the final shortlist reflects the breadth of activity across the flight simulation space, from aircraft and scenery development to tools, content creation, and long-term individual contributions. With the list of nominees now set, the next step belongs to the public. This is where YOU come in! You all are invited to review the shortlisted candidates in each category and cast your vote for the projects and individuals you believe have made the most significant impact over the past year. Each vote directly determines the final award recipients. Following your feedback from last year, we have reviewed all short-listed candidates and ensured that they either were released in 2025 or had a significant impact/update in the last calendar year. Each category listed comes with more in-depth information on the voting page should you want to review the criteria further. Furthermore, if a category is irrelevant to you, simply click ‘SKIP’ and move on. ‍Public voting will remain open until Sunday, March 1st 2026. The winners will be revealed during the Flight Sim Awards ceremony on Friday, March 20th 2026, held in cooperation with FlightSimWeekend. You can attend in person by purchasing a Captain’s Dinner ticket here: [link] The Flight Sim Awards are organised jointly by Threshold, FSElite, and Cruiselevel. The Awards aim to provide a transparent and meaningful platform to recognize excellence and contribution within the global flight simulation community. Your input is critical in ensuring that developers and products get the recognition they deserve. After countless hours of flight simulation enjoyment in 2025, this is a great opportunity to give back to the talented developers, publishers, and creators who devoted so much time to the hobby we love. The full list of nominees and the voting form are available HERE. Here’s a reminder of the categories: ‍ ‍ Stay tuned for more details on how you can watch the Flight Sim Awards ceremony at the Captains Dinner at FlightSimWeekend next month. ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft Previews Stuttgart Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has recently shared previews of their upcoming Stuttgart Airport (EDDS) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, serving the city of Stuttgart, Germany, with an average of 7 million passengers per year. Built in 1939 to replace Böblingen Airport, it was taken over by the American forces right after the war. It was partially returned in 1948: the runway and facilities were shared with the US Army during the Cold War. The original terminal was replaced in 2004 with four terminals capable of handling up to 12 million passengers annually. Eurowings and Turkish Airlines use Terminal 1; Terminal 2 is used by Lufthansa and Star Alliance partners; Terminal 3 is used by TUIfly and KLM, and Terminal 4 is a separate building for other airlines, set to be rebuilt and expanded in the future. It’s a hub for Condor, Eurowings, and TUI fly Deutschland, sporting a substantial list of destinations. Aerosoft states that the scenery is in “final approach,” progressing well through beta testing, and they plan an event to celebrate the release (apparently of never-before-seen proportions). They also state the preview screenshots do not represent the final version of the product and may be subject to changes. There’s no pricing or release date information just yet, but Threshold will keep you informed! If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Annual Navigraph FlightSim Community Survey Now Live
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. Navigraph has officially launched the FlightSim Community Survey 2026. This annual survey features a range of questions related to software, hardware, and personal preferences within the flight simulation community. It serves as a valuable resource for identifying trends and tendencies in the flight simulation world. Last year, over 23,600 simmers participated in the survey, and Navigraph hopes to exceed that number this year. The results of this survey are utilized by various developers and companies, helping to shape the future of the flight simulation industry. The survey is now open and will be available until March 9th. You can participate by clicking here. ‍ For the latest flight simulation news, remember to stay tuned on Threshold! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: WF Scenery Studio Announces Hong Kong International Airport V2 for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. WF Scenery Studio has recently announced an update to their Hong Kong International Airport (VHHH) rendition for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, featuring rebuilt models, enhanced details, and more. Hong Kong International was built to replace the old Kai Tak Airport, deemed too small to handle the traffic it was handling, and to address the insane number of complaints from airlines due to the delays, a direct consequence of the fact that it could not scale up to the demand. Planning studies started in 1974, with construction work only kicking in almost 20 years later, in 1991. Completion was achieved in early 1998. Built on a large artificial island, the total area of the airport (12.48 square kilometers) corresponds to nearly 1% of Hong Kong’s total area. ​ The airport is a hub for Cathay Pacific, Greater Bay Express, HK Express, and Hong Kong Airlines, and is visited by over 100 other airlines with more than 180 destinations. It’s also the World’s busiest cargo airport, hosting Air Hong Kong, Cathay Cargo, DHL Aviation, Hong Kong Airlines Cargo, Kalitta Air, Polar Air Cargo, and UPS Airlines. The update will feature completely rebuilt 3D models, the new terminal, which is under construction in real life, enhanced details, and extensive performance optimizations to warrant an even smoother experience. ​ After Hong Kong V2, they will focus on updating Guangzhou Baiyun International, including the new runways and satellite terminals, matching up-to-date reference imagery. ​ There’s no pricing or release date information just yet, but Threshold will keep you informed! If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: FSLabs Updates A321ceo/neo for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FSLabs has recently issued an update to their experimental branches of the A321ceo and neo for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, focusing on improving overall stability and introducing further refinements. Update v11.0.1.330 is an interim update, bringing the promised low-speed braking action refinements, updating the pedal curves with a more accurate and progressive deceleration feel, and delivering a more natural experience at speeds below 5 knots. In addition to low-speed braking, the update also brings further improvements to the systems, reflecting customer feedback and internal validation efforts. Furthermore, the FMGC code has been made more stable, and the logging architecture has been improved to make it easier to isolate and properly diagnose edge-case scenarios. There were also minor changes to ensure proper compatibility with the Sim Update 5 beta. The FSLabs A321ceo and neo are available on their website for $69.95 + VAT each. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: MK Studios Releases Key West International Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. MK Studios has recently released their rendition of Key West International Airport (KEYW) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, serving Key West, Florida, with an average of 1.5 million passengers per year. The airport’s history goes back over a century, when Augustin Parla flew to Cuba in 1913. Fifteen years later, Pan American Airways began flying scheduled routes from Key West. It was briefly taken over by the US Army during World War 2 after the Pearl Harbor attack, as an alternative to the Trumbo Point seaplane base and the main Naval Air Station for fixed-wing aircraft. ​It was returned to the city after the war, leading to its application for FAA grants in 1953 and its designation as Key West International Airport. Allegiant Air, American Airlines, American Eagle, Avelo Airlines, Breeze Airways, Delta Air Lines, Delta Connection, JetBlue, Spirit Airlines, United Airlines, and United Express serve it. The scenery features a high-detail rendition of the airport, with custom ground textures, an up-to-date ground layout, custom night lighting, custom jetways, performance-friendly optimization, and much more. It’s available on Contrail for roughly $18.86, requiring at least 2.19 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Vector Reveals Upcoming Boeing 787 Dreamliner for MSFS24
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Brand-new development studio “Vector” has recently unveiled their very first project, the Boeing 787 Dreamliner, intended to be released for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024. ‍ Vector is composed of experienced developers from the flight sim community, coming together to make what they aim to be “the best aircraft in flight simulation”. The new studio becomes another player in a growing market for Boeing airliners for the Microsoft Flight Simulator platform, joining the likes of PMDG, iFly, and Bluebird Simulations. ‍ Vector’s rendition of the Boeing 787 Dreamliner, codenamed “Project Griffin”, has been developed specifically for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, in collaboration with active Boeing 787 pilots. The team says that Project Griffin is “built for serious simulation enthusiasts”, and that their philosophy “[brings] together visuals, systems, and workflow into a cohesive experience”. Exact features and system depth have not been announced, but the team says that this will be announced “progressively as the project advances”. ‍ Vector is targeting a release date sometime this year, but more specifics have not been shared. ‍ Threshold will keep you updated on all the news regarding this exciting new product, so stay tuned! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: X-Crafts Releases ERJ Family 2.0 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. X-Crafts has released its new ERJ Family 2.0 for X-Plane 12, a rebuilt-from-scratch, new generation of its Embraer regional jets, covering the ERJ 135, ERJ 140, ERJ 145, ERJ 145XR, and the Legacy 650. The developer says the focus this time is a full, “real-world ops” systems simulation across the range, pairing a pilot-approved flight model with an authentic FMS and autopilot that supports procedures like holds, flyover waypoints, DME arcs, and intercepts. A major new addition is AirComms, an on-screen guidance system designed to keep you moving without turning the cockpit into a checklist marathon, offering reminders, warnings, and step-by-step prompts from startup to shutdown, with the option to disable it once you are comfortable. Version 2.0 also introduces a new avionics suite, with high-resolution PFD, MFD, and EICAS displays, synoptic pages, a custom navigation map, weather radar, Terrain Radar compatibility, and resizable pop-out displays. The ERJ Family 2.0 also includes a fully integrated EFB, covering doors and ground services, weight and balance with SimBrief integration, take-off and landing calculations, interactive checklists, and aircraft settings, alongside AviTab integration. X-Crafts notes that the passenger ERJs do not feature VNAV or autothrottle in real-world service, and these functions are not included here. Although some Legacy aircraft do have them, they are not currently simulated. Visually, the aircraft features a retextured interior, updated gear modelling with optional clean or dirty textures, new lighting, rain effects, VR-ready cockpit improvements, and performance optimisation via LOD tuning. X-Crafts’ ERJ Family costs USD 139.95 as a bundle, or individually at USD 69.95 (Legacy 650, E145/E145XR) and USD 59.95 (E135, E140), with an upgrade to the remaining four variants for USD 79.99. Full details are on the X-Plane.org Store product page, or click here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!
